discovery.reason: Canonical operations/action-runs/discover-skill-sources/latest.json is absent from the default branch while the discovery producer workflow and scripts exist. A bounded fallback was needed so the loop produced reviewable candidate metadata instead of status-only churn.

review.reason: Manual discovery fallback produced four metadata-only public candidates. Source review verified license, usefulness, risk, security/runtime surface, and redistribution boundaries before any normalization, catalog, package, or publication work.
review.action: Closed after creating source profiles for modelcontextprotocol/servers, vercel/ai, and openclaw/openclaw; creating a watch/license-blocked note for QuantaAlpha/GitTaskBench; opening OpenClaw high-risk source-runtime review; and opening a bounded Vercel AI SDK normalization queue.

normalization.reason: Vercel AI SDK source review found Apache-2.0 license, high usefulness, and direct package-facing value for provider abstraction and sandbox/tool safety guidance. Normalization had to be original and could not copy examples or implementation content.

catalog.reason: The risk review changed package-facing skill metadata/body for dist/skills/auditing-mcp-servers-for-tool-poisoning/SKILL.md: risk_level high, requires_review true, domain security-defensive, review gate, and redacted default probing detail. Generated catalog surfaces required refresh or verification before package/publication endorsement.
catalog.action: Closed after catalog/install-manifest parity was verified in reports/catalog/2026-07-08-risk-review-catalog-parity.md. No npm publication occurred.

catalog.reason: A new normalized dist skill was added from the reviewed Vercel AI SDK source profile. Catalog/install-manifest parity must be verified before metadata backlog cleanup, package endorsement, npm publication, registry publication, or installable-surface claims.
catalog.action: Verify or refresh generated catalog surfaces and record whether skill count, software-engineering count, medium-risk count, requires_review metadata, and installer selection fields are coherent. Do not publish to npm.

risk.reason: Package-facing uncategorized skill included live MCP server probing, SSRF target testing, unauthenticated endpoint checks, and tool-description prompt-injection handling. It required review/redaction before routine metadata classification, manual discovery expansion, catalog endorsement, package endorsement, or publication endorsement.
risk.action: Closed after package-facing skill was converted to a high-risk authorized-use defensive wrapper and risk report was created.

risk.reason: OpenClaw has MIT license and high usefulness, but source review found high-risk real messaging/account/channel integrations, browser/canvas/nodes/cron tools, gateway exposure, host tool access, daemon/onboarding flow, and sandbox policy surfaces. Safe abstraction boundaries had to be reviewed before any OpenClaw-derived normalization.
risk.action: Closed after defining a conditional safe boundary: OpenClaw may only inform generic original runtime-surface safety review patterns. Commands, account setup, channel mutation, browser automation, cron execution, gateway setup, daemon/onboarding detail, and implementation content remain blocked.

critic.reason: Catalog still has a large uncategorized-domain and unspecified-risk backlog under the current quality gate.
critic.action: Continue bounded metadata backfill batches only after discovery/source-review and higher-priority risk/normalization/catalog gates allow it.
